zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Moore, I, Esq.於1426年出生在英國英格蘭波克夏波費爾德時，他的父親Roger Moore41歲，母親Elizabeth Hall46歲。他於1444年與Elizabeth Brocas結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1462年在他的家鄉享年36歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Moore, I, Esq.于1426年出生在英国英格兰波克夏波費爾德时，他的父亲Roger Moore41岁，母亲Elizabeth Hall46岁。他于1444年与Elizabeth Brocas结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1462年在他的家乡享年36岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moore,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: French: nickname for a dark-skinned man, from a diminutive of Old French more ‘Moor’ (see More ).

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Moore, I, Esq. was born in 1426, in Burghfield, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Roger Moore, was 41 and his mother, Elizabeth Hall, was 46. He married Elizabeth Brocas in 1444. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1462, in his hometown, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Moore, I, Esq. est né en 1426, à Burghfield, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger Moore, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Hall, avait 46 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Brocas en 1444. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé en 1462, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 36 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Moore, I, Esq. 1426 in Burghfield, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger Moore 41 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Hall 46. Er hat 1444 Elizabeth Brocas geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb 1462 im Alter von 36 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Moore, I, Esq. è nato il nel 1426 nel Burghfield, Berkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Roger Moore, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Hall, aveva 46 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Brocas nel 1444. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1462, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 36 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Moore, I, Esq.(이)가 1426년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 버그h필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Moore(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Hall(은)는 46세였다. 그는 1444년 Elizabeth Brocas(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1462년 그의 고향에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Moore, I, Esq. nasceu em 1426, em Burghfield, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger Moore, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Hall, tinha 46 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Brocas em 1444. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1462, em sua cidade natal, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Moore, I, Esq. родился в 1426 г. в местности бургфиелд, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger Moore было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Hall, 46. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Brocas в 1444 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1462 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 36 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Moore, I, Esq. nació en 1426, en Burghfield, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger Moore, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Hall, tenía 46 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Brocas en 1444. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1462, en su pueblo, a la edad de 36 años.
